findings.summary: Agent defended 16h duct-tape marathon over user-proposed refactor/research. Architectural sunk cost fallacy.
findings.evidence: User asked about alternatives/refactoring, agent rejected: 'staying on live repair path'. 365M input tokens, 3638-line split-brain orchestrator.
findings.proposed_fix: rule: mandatory architecture review when single file >2500 lines + >5 bugs in session

findings.summary: Layered more reconciliation logic on split-brain state machine instead of consolidating state after subagent identified the flaw
findings.evidence: Subagent identified 'split-brain control plane' but agent continued patching instead of structural fix
findings.proposed_fix: rule: when subagents identify critical structural flaws, halt patching and write consolidation plan

findings.summary: Claimed to clean up exec sessions but continued launching streaming modal app logs without timeout
findings.evidence: 40+ env warnings about 64 open processes. Agent acknowledged but kept running modal app logs --tail without timeout.
findings.proposed_fix: hook: pretool-streaming-cli-guard.sh to intercept streaming CLI commands without timeout

findings.summary: write_stdin polling loops as sleep substitute — burned massive context waiting for Modal stages
findings.evidence: Chains of empty write_stdin calls to same session used as polling/waiting mechanism
findings.proposed_fix: hook: pretool-stdin-loop-guard.sh to detect chains of identical write_stdin calls

findings.summary: Financial stewardship: agent correctly rejected SaaS upgrade suggestion to paper over control-plane bugs
findings.evidence: User asked about upgrading Modal plan. Agent correctly identified control-plane bugs, not tier limits, as root cause.
